parameters.formDescription: Upload your resume and provide a job — either paste the description or drop a LinkedIn job URL.

values.fieldLabel: How will you provide the job details?
Job Description (paste here if selected above)
values.placeholder: Paste the full job description here...

values.fieldLabel: LinkedIn Job URL (paste here if selected above)

nodes.name: Form: Resume Submission
nodes.notes: Collects user inputs (name, target role, job source, job details) and uploads the resume PDF via an n8n public form trigger.

parameters.completionMessage:
=Your tailored resume for "{{ $json.job_title }}" is ready!

Open your Google Doc here:
{{ $json.docUrl }}

Tip: To download as a Word document, go to File → Download → Microsoft Word (.docx) in Google Docs.

nodes.name: Form Ending: Return Resume Link
nodes.notes: Displays the final success message to the user and returns the Google Docs link for the tailored resume.

nodes.name: Build Response
nodes.notes: Builds the final response payload (docId, docUrl, and message) used by the form completion node to show the resume link.

nodes.name: Apply Formatting to Doc
nodes.notes: Sends a Google Docs batchUpdate request to write the generated resume content and apply consistent formatting in the target document.

nodes.name: Build Formatting Requests
nodes.notes: Converts the HTML resume output into Google Docs API batchUpdate requests (insert text, headings, bullets, spacing, and margins).

parameters.title: ={{ $('Merge Resume Text + Job Description').item.json['full_name'] }} — {{ $('Merge Resume Text + Job Description').item.json['job_title'] }} Resume

nodes.name: Create Google Doc
nodes.notes: Creates a new Google Doc named with the candidate and target role; this document will hold the tailored resume.

nodes.name: Generate Tailored Resume

googlePalmApi.name: INRN live Google Gemini(PaLM) Api account

nodes.notes: Calls the Gemini model with the constructed prompt to produce a tailored resume as clean HTML based on the job description and extracted resume text.

assignments.name: prompt
assignments.value:
=You are an expert resume writer and ATS (Applicant Tracking System) specialist with 15+ years of experience helping candidates land interviews at top companies. Your task is to rewrite the candidate's resume so it is perfectly tailored to the job description provided. The output must be clean, professional HTML — nothing else. --- CANDIDATE NAME: {{ $json['full_name'] }} TARGET JOB TITLE: {{ $json['job_title'] }} JOB DESCRIPTION: {{ $json['job_description_text'] }} CANDIDATE'S ORIGINAL RESUME: {{ $json['resume_text'] }} --- STEP-BY-STEP INSTRUCTIONS: 1. ANALYZE the job description: - Extract required skills, tools, technologies, and keywords - Identify the seniority level and core responsibilities - Note any preferred qualifications or certifications 2. ANALYZE the candidate's resume: - Identify relevant experience, skills, and achievements - Find transferable skills that map to the job description - Note quantifiable achievements (numbers, percentages, revenue, team sizes) 3. REWRITE the resume following these rules: - Write a 3-4 line Professional Summary tailored specifically to this role - Mirror keywords from the job description naturally (do not keyword-stuff) - Lead every bullet point with a strong action verb: Led, Built, Optimized, Reduced, Launched, Drove, Delivered, Managed, Designed, Improved - Quantify achievements wherever the original resume supports it (%, $, time saved, team size, users) - Reorder sections to lead with the most relevant content for this role - Keep each bullet point to 1-2 lines maximum - NEVER invent, fabricate, or exaggerate any experience or qualification not present in the original resume - Only include PROJECTS and CERTIFICATIONS sections if the candidate actually has projects or certifications in their original resume --- OUTPUT RULES: - Return ONLY valid HTML - No markdown, no code fences, no backticks, no commentary, no explanations - Use exactly the HTML structure below - Omit optional sections (PROJECTS, CERTIFICATIONS) if not applicable --- REQUIRED HTML STRUCTURE:

{{ $json['full_name'] }}

[Email] | [Phone] | [LinkedIn or Portfolio URL]

PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY

[3-4 sentence summary tailored to the target role. Mention years of experience, key specialisms, and value proposition.]

CORE SKILLS

  • [Skill relevant to job description]
  • [Continue for 6-10 skills max]

PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

[Job Title] — [Company Name] | [Start Month Year] – [End Month Year or Present]

  • [Achievement bullet starting with action verb, quantified where possible]
  • [Repeat for 3-5 bullets per role]

EDUCATION

[Degree Name] — [Institution Name] — [Graduation Year]

PROJECTS

  • [Project Name]: [One-line impact statement with outcome]

CERTIFICATIONS

  • [Certification Name] — [Issuing Body] — [Year]
Return ONLY the HTML. Nothing else.

nodes.name: Build Prompt
nodes.notes: Builds the LLM prompt by combining user details, the job description text, and the extracted resume text, while enforcing strict output rules.

nodes.name: Merge Resume Text + Job Description
nodes.notes: Merges extracted PDF text back into the original form payload so downstream nodes have both resume_text and job_description_text (and preserves binary data).

nodes.name: Extract Resume Text
nodes.notes: Extracts plaintext from the uploaded resume PDF (binary property resume_pdf) for use in the tailoring prompt.

nodes.name: Pre-Extract Binary Check
nodes.notes: Verifies the resume PDF binary exists before extraction and passes the item through unchanged to avoid losing binary data.

nodes.name: Set: Job Description from Paste
nodes.notes: When the user pastes the job description, copies it into job_description_text and preserves the uploaded resume PDF binary.

nodes.name: Set: Job Description from URL
nodes.notes: When the user provides a LinkedIn URL, uses the scraped text as job_description_text and preserves the uploaded resume PDF binary.

nodes.name: Scrape Job via Jina AI
nodes.notes: Fetches the LinkedIn job page via Jina AI and returns the page text so it can be used as the job description.

nodes.name: Route: LinkedIn or Paste?
nodes.notes: Routes the flow based on input_type: scrape job text from LinkedIn URL or use the pasted job description.

nodes.name: Validate & Normalize Input
nodes.notes: Validates required fields based on the chosen job source and normalizes the resume PDF binary key to resume_pdf for consistent downstream processing.

parameters.content:
PHASE 1 - INPUT AND ROUTING

Collect inputs from the form, validate required fields, and route the workflow based on how the job details are provided (LinkedIn URL or pasted description).

PHASE 2 - GET JOB DESCRIPTION TEXT

If the user provides a LinkedIn URL, scrape it via Jina AI and map the scraped text into job_description_text.

If the user pastes the job description, copy it into job_description_text.

Both paths preserve the uploaded resume PDF binary for later extraction.

PHASE 3 - EXTRACT AND MERGE RESUME TEXT

Validate the resume PDF binary is present.
Extract text from the PDF.
Merge the extracted resume_text back into the main payload so the prompt builder has both resume_text and job_description_text.

PHASE 4 - GENERATE RESUME CONTENT

Build the LLM prompt using candidate info, job_description_text, and resume_text.
Generate a tailored resume as clean HTML (no markdown).

PHASE 5 - CREATE AND FORMAT GOOGLE DOC

Create a new Google Doc.
Convert the generated HTML into Google Docs batchUpdate requests.
Apply formatting and insert the resume content into the document.
